html:
Exclusive to:
Only during the Event: Winter's Feast

“Can be unwrapped to release items inside.”

-

The Gifts work exactly like Bundled Supplies, containing up to 4 stacks of items. They can be created by players using Gift Wraps and vary in sizes and colors. Unlike Bundling Wraps, Gifts do not return the Gift Wrap's crafting materials when opened.

During Winter's Feast, opening a Loot Stash will drop 6 Gifts instead of 4 Bundled Supplies. The 2 extra Gifts will contain Electric Milk, Winter Food and Winter's Feast Ornaments.

Also Gifts can be found near wild Winter's Feast Trees and most of them contains Hounds

Gift Wrap

“Can package up to 4 stacks of items into one item that can be unpacked later.”

-

The Gift Wraps work exactly like Bundling Wraps, except their crafting recipe is much cheaper and it gives 4 Gift Wraps per craft. Gift Wraps are found in the Storage Solutions Filter and crafted from 1 Papyrus and 1 Petals. They do not require prototyping. Perishable items stop spoiling when wrapped into a Gift Wrap. Some items are not allowed to be put in a Gift Wrap, for example Chester's Eye Bone, Star-Sky or the Stag Antler.

Gallery

  • Several gifts of different sizes placed around a Fire Pit.
  • Winter Food used to decorate a Winter's Feast Tree.
Winter's FeastCraftable Items

“Is that for me?”
–Wilson
“It's for me right?!”
–Willow
“Is nice little present!”
–Wolfgang
“The only gift I desire is a swift end...”
–Wendy
“MINIONS. ARE YOU FINALLY RECOGNIZING MY NATURAL EMINENCE?”
–WX-78
“Oh goodness, how lovely!”
–Wickerbottom
“They're just as fun to give as they are to receive.”
–Woodie
“An elegantly wrapped social obligation.”
–Maxwell
“A gift! Fröm... the göds?”
–Wigfrid
“Oh! What is it?!”
–Webber
“Pour moi?”
–Warly
“Give to friends!”
–Wormwood
“My presence is a gift. Ha.”
–Winona
“Presents! How fun!”
–Wortox
“Present for me!”
–Wurt
“A present!”
–Walter
“Oh! Uh... was I supposed to bring a present too?”
–Wanda
“Can be unwrapped to release items inside.”
-
The Gifts work exactly like Bundled Supplies, containing up to 4 stacks of items. They can be created by players using Gift Wraps and vary in sizes and colors. Unlike Bundling Wraps, Gifts do not return the Gift Wrap's crafting materials when opened.
During Winter's Feast, opening a Loot Stash will drop 6 Gifts instead of 4 Bundled Supplies. The 2 extra Gifts will contain Electric Milk, Winter Food and Winter's Feast Ornaments.
Also Gifts can be found near wild Winter's Feast Trees and most of them contains Hounds

“That's a wrap!”
–Wilson
“Ribbons burn real good!”
–Willow
“Wolfgang needs help tying little bow.”
–Wolfgang
“Abby... how do I make the ribbon curly?”
–Wendy
“I AM THE SUPERIOR GIFT GIVER”
–WX-78
“Wrapping presents is soothing work, isn't it?”
–Wickerbottom
“Should we do something nice today, Lucy?”
–Woodie
“For wrapping odds and ends...”
–Maxwell
“I must shöw my allies höw much they are valued!”
–Wigfrid
“I want to give someone something nice!”
–Webber
“I could hide some cookies inside for the little ones.”
–Warly
“Fun wrapping time!”
–Wormwood
“I could wrap stuff up real nice.”
–Winona
“That's a wrap!”
–Wortox
“Why scale-less wrap things in more things?”
–Wurt
“I'm not great at wrapping things... it always ends up a crumpled ball.”
–Walter
“Wouldn't it be quicker to just put it in a nice bag?”
–Wanda
“Can package up to 4 stacks of items into one item that can be unpacked later.”
-
The Gift Wraps work exactly like Bundling Wraps, except their crafting recipe is much cheaper and it gives 4 Gift Wraps per craft. Gift Wraps are found in the Storage Solutions Filter and crafted from 1 Papyrus and 1 Petals. They do not require prototyping. Perishable items stop spoiling when wrapped into a Gift Wrap. Some items are not allowed to be put in a Gift Wrap, for example Chester's Eye Bone, Star-Sky or the Stag Antler.

Also [[Gift]]s can be found near wild [[Winter's Feast Tree]]s and most of them contains [[Hound]]s
